title: Fluffy Caramelized Dutch Potato Hash
description: A salty, aerated, and delicious Dutch dish featuring caramelized potatoes and unsalted butter, perfect for a macrobiotic-friendly brunch
instructions: In a large bowl, combine the grated potatoes, onion, garlic, salt, and black pepper, and mix well.
Heat 2 tablespoons of unsalted butter in a large non-stick skillet over medium heat.
Add the potato mixture to the skillet and press down with a spatula to form a flat layer.
Cook for 10 minutes or until the bottom is golden brown and crispy.
Flip the potato hash over and cook for an additional 5 minutes or until the other side is also golden brown.
While the potato hash is cooking, heat the remaining 2 tablespoons of unsalted butter in a small saucepan over low heat.
Once the butter has melted, add the beaten eggs to the saucepan and scramble them until they are cooked through.
To serve, place the caramelized potato hash on a plate, top with the scrambled eggs, and sprinkle with chopped fresh parsley.
